wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the ECS Creator platform. Explain the project's purpose as a cloud infrastructure management system for Alibaba Cloud ECS instances. Document prerequisites including Python, Node.js, Docker, and Alibaba Cloud account setup. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for both local development and production deployment using Docker Compose. Include environment configuration examples, database setup, and initial user creation. Demonstrate basic usage patterns including login, creating your first ECS instance through templates, and understanding the approval workflow. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for beginners.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the FastAPI backend system. Describe the modular architecture with clear separation of concerns including routers, services, models, and middleware layers. Document the application initialization process in main.py, configuration management patterns, database connection handling, and authentication middleware implementation. Explain the request processing flow from HTTP endpoints through validation, business logic to data access. Detail the service layer abstraction pattern, dependency injection, and error handling strategies. Include component interaction diagrams showing how routers call services, services interact with models, and external API integrations are handled.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Structure & Core Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the React application structure and core components. Explain the main application bootstrap process in main.jsx, the root App component architecture with routing setup, and global styling configuration. Document the component hierarchy, state management patterns, and how the application initializes services and middleware. Include examples of component composition patterns, error boundaries implementation, and performance optimization strategies. Address the CSS architecture using Tailwind CSS utility classes and global style organization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Application Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core FastAPI application components. Explain the application initialization process in main.py, including middleware setup, dependency injection configuration, and route registration patterns. Document the centralized configuration management system in config.py with environment-specific settings, secret handling, and runtime configuration overrides. Detail the database connectivity implementation in database.py covering connection pooling, session management, and error handling strategies. Include practical examples of extending the application with new middleware, configuring additional services, and implementing custom dependency injection patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: ECS Service Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Alibaba Cloud ECS service integration. Detail the complete ECS instance lifecycle management including creation, configuration, monitoring, and termination operations. Document all available ECS instance types, specifications, and pricing considerations. Explain the template-based provisioning system that maps application requirements to cloud resources. Include error handling strategies, retry mechanisms, and timeout configurations for API calls. Provide examples of common ECS operations like starting/stopping instances, modifying configurations, and managing snapshots. Address security group integration, key pair management, and resource tagging strategies. Document performance optimization techniques and cost control measures for ECS resource management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication endpoints. Document the login endpoint with username/password authentication, JWT token generation and refresh mechanisms. Detail logout functionality and session management. Include user profile retrieval and update operations. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods (POST /api/v1/auth/login, POST /api/v1/auth/refresh, GET /api/v1/auth/me), request/response schemas with field types and validation rules, authentication requirements using Bearer tokens, and status codes (200, 401, 403, 422). Provide concrete examples of request payloads including JSON format for login credentials and response structures containing JWT tokens and user information. Document error handling patterns for invalid credentials, expired tokens, and security violations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Containerization & Docker
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive containerization documentation for the ECS Creator platform. Document the complete Docker setup including multi-stage builds, image optimization, and security best practices. Explain the Docker Compose orchestration with service dependencies, networking, and volume management. Detail the backend and frontend Dockerfile configurations, environment variable handling, and health check implementations. Include container lifecycle management, logging strategies, and resource constraints. Provide guidance on building optimized production images, managing secrets in containers, and implementing proper container security scanning.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Initialization & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the FastAPI application initialization and configuration management. Detail the application startup process in main.py including middleware registration, CORS configuration, exception handlers, and API versioning setup. Document the centralized configuration system in config.py covering environment variable handling, database connection parameters, cloud service credentials, and security settings. Explain dependency injection patterns, lifespan events, and graceful shutdown procedures. Include practical examples of adding new middleware, configuring third-party services, and managing environment-specific settings.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Bootstrap & Entry Points
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the React application bootstrap process and entry points. Document how main.jsx initializes the React application, sets up the root component, and configures global providers. Explain the App.jsx component structure, routing configuration, authentication guards, and application initialization flow. Include details about dependency injection, context providers, error boundaries, and performance optimizations during startup. Provide examples of customizing the bootstrap process and adding global middleware.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Portal Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the user portal interface components. Document the main UserPortal component architecture, including resource management features, template selection and usage, request submission workflows, and active resource monitoring from a user perspective. Explain the component structure, state management patterns, API integration for user-specific operations, and user experience flows. Include examples of how users interact with ECS instances, submit provisioning requests, and monitor their resources through the self-service interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Models (User, Session, Settings)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for core system models including User, Session, and Settings. Detail entity relationships, field definitions, data types, and constraints for authentication and system configuration. Document primary/foreign key relationships, indexes, and database optimization strategies. Explain user role-based access control implementation, session management lifecycle, and application settings persistence. Include validation rules, business logic constraints, and security considerations for sensitive data handling. Provide sample data structures and common query patterns for user administration and system configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Middleware
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the authentication middleware implementation. Detail the JWT token validation process, including token extraction from headers, signature verification, and expiration handling. Document the user context injection mechanism that populates request context with authenticated user information. Explain role-based access control (RBAC) implementation, permission checking decorators, and how to implement custom authorization rules. Include practical examples of protecting API endpoints, implementing admin-only routes, and extending permission checks. Address security considerations such as token storage, CSRF protection, and session management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cloud Integration Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Alibaba Cloud integration services. Detail the ECS service implementation including instance provisioning, configuration management, lifecycle operations (create, start, stop, terminate), and status monitoring. Document the VPC service covering network setup, subnet management, security groups, and routing configuration. Include concrete examples of service method invocation, error handling patterns, retry mechanisms, and rate limiting strategies. Address authentication with Alibaba Cloud SDK, credential management, and API response parsing. Provide guidance on extending cloud integrations and handling asynchronous operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Flow & Token Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the JWT-based authentication flow and token management system. Detail the complete authentication lifecycle from user login through token generation, validation, and refresh mechanisms. Explain the middleware implementation for request interception, token extraction, and user context injection into request scope. Document the schema definitions for authentication requests and responses, including error handling patterns. Include practical examples of implementing protected endpoints, custom authentication decorators, and extending the authentication flow with additional providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for user management endpoints. Document all HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) for user CRUD operations including user registration, profile updates, role management, and user status control. Detail request/response schemas with field validation rules, authentication requirements, and authorization levels. Include practical examples of user creation workflows, bulk operations, and search/filtering capabilities. Document error responses, validation errors, and common integration patterns for user administration tasks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Connection & Session Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the database connection management and session handling system. Explain the SQLAlchemy engine configuration, connection pooling setup, and session lifecycle management. Document the database URL configuration, connection parameters, and environment-specific settings. Include examples of proper session usage patterns, transaction management, and error handling strategies. Address connection recycling, timeout configurations, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cloud Services (ECS & VPC)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Alibaba Cloud integration services. Detail the ECS instance management service including creation, configuration, monitoring, and lifecycle operations. Document the VPC service covering network setup, security groups, and resource isolation. Explain error handling strategies, retry mechanisms, rate limiting implementation, and authentication with Alibaba Cloud SDK. Include practical examples of creating ECS instances, managing VPC networks, handling API responses, and implementing proper error recovery patterns. Address performance optimization, connection pooling, and monitoring cloud service calls.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the user management endpoints. Document all HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for user CRUD operations, profile management, and role-based access control. Include detailed examples of user registration, login, password updates, and administrative user operations. Explain the integration with JWT authentication middleware and permission validation. Address error handling, input validation, and security considerations specific to user data management.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the reusable UI components library. Document all shared components including buttons, forms, modals, tables, and navigation elements. For each component, specify props/attributes, default values, event handlers, and styling options. Include usage examples with code snippets showing proper implementation patterns. Document component composition patterns, accessibility features, and responsive design considerations. Explain how to extend existing components and create custom variants while maintaining consistency with the design system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Template entity. Detail the template structure including resource specifications, configuration parameters, and validation rules. Document template fields such as instance type, storage requirements, network settings, and security configurations. Explain template versioning, sharing mechanisms, and inheritance patterns. Include examples of template definitions and their corresponding database schema. Document template validation logic, default value handling, and compatibility checks with Alibaba Cloud ECS specifications. Address template lifecycle management, caching strategies for performance optimization, and audit trail integration for template changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: ECS Service Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Alibaba Cloud ECS service implementation. Document all ECS instance lifecycle operations including create_instance, start_instance, stop_instance, terminate_instance, and get_instance_status methods. Explain instance configuration parameters such as instance type, image selection, security groups, and network settings. Include concrete examples of method invocation with proper error handling, retry mechanisms, and timeout management. Document authentication setup with Alibaba Cloud SDK, credential configuration, and API response parsing. Address rate limiting considerations, async operation handling, and resource cleanup patterns. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common ECS API errors and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the ECS Creator platform. Describe the high-level system design showing the separation between FastAPI backend, React frontend, PostgreSQL database, and Nginx reverse proxy. Document the containerized microservices architecture using Docker Compose. Explain component interactions, data flows from user requests through authentication middleware to service layer and external Alibaba Cloud APIs. Detail the technology stack decisions, system boundaries, and integration patterns. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and deployment topology diagrams. Address cross-cutting concerns like security, monitoring, and error handling across all components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the React frontend application. Describe the component-based architecture with clear separation between UI components, page components, and service layers. Document the application bootstrap process, routing structure, state management patterns, and API client implementation. Explain the component hierarchy from App.jsx down to reusable UI components, data flow patterns, and integration with the backend API. Detail the build configuration using Vite, styling approach with Tailwind CSS, and development workflow. Include component interaction diagrams and data binding patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Component Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the reusable UI component library built with React and Tailwind CSS. Document each component's props, events, slots, and customization options. Include usage examples with code snippets demonstrating common patterns like form inputs, buttons, modals, tables, and navigation elements. Provide guidelines for creating new components following established patterns, accessibility compliance, responsive design considerations, and theming support. Address component composition patterns and integration with the rest of the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the authentication and security subsystem. Thoroughly explain the JWT-based authentication middleware implementation including token validation, user context injection, and role-based access control mechanisms. Document the authentication schemas for request/response validation, password hashing algorithms, and cryptographic utilities. Detail the auth router endpoints covering login/logout flows, token refresh mechanisms, and user profile management. Include concrete examples of implementing custom authentication decorators, extending permission checks, and integrating with external identity providers. Address security best practices, common vulnerabilities, and their mitigations.

wiki_catalogs.name: VPC Network Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Alibaba Cloud VPC network integration services. Explain virtual private cloud configuration including subnet management, route tables, and network ACLs. Document security group operations for firewall rules and access control policies. Detail NAT gateway configuration for outbound internet access and port forwarding. Include VPN connection setup for hybrid cloud scenarios and cross-region networking. Provide examples of network topology design patterns and best practices for production deployments. Address network performance optimization, bandwidth management, and monitoring strategies. Document troubleshooting techniques for common networking issues and connectivity problems. Include cost optimization recommendations for VPC resources and data transfer costs.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for user management endpoints. Document CRUD operations for user administration including create, read, update, and delete users. Detail role-based access control endpoints for assigning admin and user roles. Include user search and filtering capabilities. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods (GET /api/v1/users, POST /api/v1/users, PUT /api/v1/users/{id}, DELETE /api/v1/users/{id}), URL patterns with path parameters, request/response schemas with user model fields (username, email, role, status), authentication requirements with admin privileges, and appropriate status codes. Provide examples of user creation requests with role assignments, bulk operations, and pagination responses. Document validation rules, input sanitization, and error responses for duplicate users or insufficient permissions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Nginx Reverse Proxy Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed Nginx reverse proxy configuration documentation for the ECS Creator platform. Document the complete nginx.conf setup including load balancing, SSL/TLS termination, caching strategies, and security headers. Explain request routing to backend services, static file serving for the frontend, and WebSocket support for real-time features. Include performance tuning parameters, rate limiting configuration, and access control mechanisms. Provide troubleshooting guides for common proxy issues, monitoring setup, and log analysis techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the JWT-based authentication and authorization system. Explain the middleware implementation for token validation, user context injection, and role-based access control. Document the authentication flow from login through token generation, refresh mechanisms, and session management. Detail password hashing with bcrypt, cryptographic utilities, and security best practices. Include schema definitions for auth requests/responses, error handling strategies, and integration patterns with external authentication providers. Provide examples of implementing custom middleware, protecting endpoints, and extending permission systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture & Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the React component architecture and structural patterns. Document the separation between reusable UI components in components/ui.jsx and page-specific components. Explain the component hierarchy, prop drilling vs context usage, composition patterns, and component lifecycle management. Detail the layout components like AdminLayout.jsx and their role in organizing page structures. Include guidelines for creating new components, naming conventions, and best practices for component reusability and maintainability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Layout & Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the admin panel layout and navigation system. Document the AdminLayout component structure, sidebar navigation implementation, role-based access control for administrative features, and responsive design considerations. Explain how the layout manages different admin sections, handles authentication checks, and provides consistent navigation across all administrative interfaces. Include examples of adding new admin pages and implementing permission-based visibility controls.

wiki_catalogs.name: Resource Management Models (Template, Request, ActiveResource)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data model documentation for resource management entities including Template, Request, and ActiveResource models. Document the template-based provisioning workflow, request lifecycle states, and active resource tracking. Detail entity relationships showing how templates define resource configurations, requests represent provisioning attempts, and active resources track deployed instances. Explain state transitions, validation rules for resource specifications, and audit trail integration. Include database schema diagrams showing the relationship between templates, requests, and cloud resources. Document performance considerations for large-scale resource management and caching strategies for frequently accessed templates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Schemas
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for authentication-related Pydantic schemas. Document the LoginRequest schema for user authentication, including username/password validation rules and error handling. Detail the TokenResponse schema for JWT token responses, including token structure and expiration fields. Explain UserSchema for user profile data, including field constraints and serialization rules. Document password validation schemas, email validation patterns, and input sanitization. Include examples of schema usage in API endpoints, custom validators, and integration with FastAPI's request/response validation system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Approval Workflow Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the approval workflow engine. Explain the request routing system, state machine transitions, and multi-level approval chains. Document the notification system integration, escalation rules, and audit trail generation. Include concrete examples of workflow definition, approval chain configuration, and custom approval logic implementation. Address concurrent approval handling, timeout management, and workflow rollback scenarios. Provide guidance on extending workflows with new approval types and integrating external approval systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authorization & Role-Based Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the role-based access control (RBAC) system and authorization mechanisms. Explain how user roles and permissions are defined, stored, and validated throughout the application. Document the permission checking middleware, decorator patterns for endpoint protection, and hierarchical role structures. Include examples of implementing custom permission rules, dynamic authorization logic, and integrating with external authorization systems. Address common authorization patterns like resource-based permissions and contextual access control.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for template management endpoints. Document HTTP methods for creating, reading, updating, deleting, and versioning resource templates. Explain template schema validation, parameter inheritance, and template sharing mechanisms. Include examples of template creation workflows, version comparison, and batch template operations. Document template validation rules, dependency management, and testing capabilities. Address template lifecycle management and rollback procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the core business data models including User, Template, and Request entities. Detail the schema definitions, field constraints, validation rules, and relationships between models. Document primary keys, foreign key relationships, indexes, and database constraints. Include examples of model instantiation, relationship navigation, and common query patterns. Explain business logic enforcement at the model level and data integrity rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Workflow & Approval Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the approval workflow engine and business process management. Explain the workflow state machine, approval chain logic, and notification system integration. Detail how requests are created, routed through approval hierarchies, and tracked throughout their lifecycle. Include examples of configuring approval workflows, handling approval decisions, implementing escalation rules, and managing workflow exceptions. Address concurrent approval processing, audit trail maintenance, and workflow analytics. Provide guidance on extending the workflow engine for custom approval processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the template management endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas for creating, updating, deleting, and retrieving resource templates. Include examples of template versioning, sharing mechanisms, and validation rules. Explain the relationship between templates and ECS instance provisioning. Address template permissions, collaboration features, and integration with the approval workflow system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Components & Layouts
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for page-level components and layout structures. Document the AdminLayout.jsx component that provides the administrative interface framework, including sidebar navigation, header components, and content area management. Explain the UserPortal.jsx structure for user-facing functionality and the Login.jsx authentication flow. Detail routing integration, state management at page level, data fetching patterns, and error handling strategies. Include guidelines for creating new pages and maintaining consistent layouts across different user roles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Request Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data model documentation for the Request entity representing resource provisioning attempts. Document request lifecycle states from submission through approval to deployment completion. Detail request fields including user association, template reference, approval chain status, and execution results. Explain state transition rules, validation constraints, and error handling mechanisms. Include examples of request workflows showing different approval scenarios and failure cases. Document request auditing capabilities, status tracking, and notification triggers. Address concurrent request handling, timeout management, and rollback procedures for failed deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: VPC Service Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Alibaba Cloud VPC service implementation. Document virtual private cloud operations including create_vpc, create_subnet, configure_security_groups, and manage_network_routing. Explain network topology configuration, CIDR block management, and subnet allocation strategies. Include concrete examples of VPC setup workflows, security group rule management, and network connectivity testing. Document integration with ECS instances for network attachment and IP address management. Address error handling for network conflicts, quota limits, and availability zone constraints. Provide guidance on network monitoring, troubleshooting connectivity issues, and optimizing network performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend development documentation for the FastAPI application. Explain the modular architecture with clear separation of concerns between routers, services, models, and schemas. Document the application initialization process, configuration management, and database connectivity patterns. Detail the authentication middleware implementation using JWT tokens and role-based access control. Include code examples from actual implementation showing request processing flow, error handling strategies, and dependency injection patterns. Address testing approaches, logging implementation, and performance optimization techniques specific to the backend components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database architecture documentation for the PostgreSQL schema design. Detail the entity relationship model including User, Template, Request, Approval, AuditLog, and ActiveResource entities. Document table structures, foreign key relationships, indexes, and constraints. Explain the ORM mapping strategy using SQLAlchemy, migration management with Alembic, and data access patterns. Include ER diagrams showing relationships between entities, data flow patterns, and query optimization strategies. Address data integrity rules, transaction handling, and performance considerations for high-volume operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Service Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the API service layer implementation handling HTTP communication with the backend. Document the API client configuration, request/response interceptors, authentication token management, and error handling strategies. Include examples of making API calls, handling different response types, implementing retry logic, and managing loading states. Address security considerations, rate limiting, caching strategies, and debugging techniques for API interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models & Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for all SQLAlchemy ORM models. Detail entity relationships, field definitions, data types, and constraints for User, Template, Request, AuditLog, Session, and Settings models. Document primary/foreign key relationships, indexes, and database optimization strategies. Explain data validation rules, business logic constraints, and lifecycle hooks. Address data access patterns, caching considerations, and performance optimization techniques specific to each model.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for template management endpoints. Document template CRUD operations for creating, reading, updating, and deleting resource templates. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods (GET /api/v1/templates, POST /api/v1/templates, PUT /api/v1/templates/{id}, DELETE /api/v1/templates/{id}), URL patterns with template identifiers, request/response schemas with template definitions including ECS specifications, VPC configurations, and metadata fields. Authentication requirements should distinguish between user and admin access levels. Provide examples of template creation with cloud resource specifications, version comparison responses, and template deployment previews. Document template validation errors, permission checks, and audit logging for template modifications.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive environment configuration documentation for the ECS Creator platform. Include environment-specific overrides, secret management strategies, and configuration validation procedures. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the application settings management service and configuration handling. Explain how system settings are stored, retrieved, validated, and cached. Address settings versioning, migration strategies, and environment-specific configurations. wiki_catalogs.name: Request Workflow API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the resource request workflow endpoints. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the ActiveResource entity tracking deployed cloud instances. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend development documentation for the React-based single-page application. Detail the API service layer implementation for HTTP communication with the backend. Include examples of page components for both user portal and admin interfaces. Address build configuration with Vite, styling approach with Tailwind CSS, and responsive design patterns. Provide guidelines for adding new pages, components, and integrating with existing services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Topology
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create deployment architecture documentation for the containerized microservices setup. Describe the Docker Compose orchestration showing service dependencies, network topology, and volume management. Document the Nginx reverse proxy configuration for load balancing, SSL termination, and static file serving. Explain the containerization strategy for both backend and frontend applications, environment variable management, and health check implementations. Include infrastructure diagrams showing service communication patterns, data persistence strategies, and scaling considerations. Address production deployment patterns, monitoring integration, and disaster recovery procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Components & Routing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for all page components and routing structure. Document the user portal interface for resource management, admin panel layout and navigation, and individual administrative pages including user management, template administration, approval workflows, active resource monitoring, audit logging, and system settings. Include examples of page component architecture, data fetching patterns, state management within pages, and navigation between different sections. Address role-based access control implementation and conditional rendering based on user permissions.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Routers & Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API router documentation covering all RESTful endpoints. For each router module, document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Explain parameter validation, error handling strategies, and response formatting patterns. Include practical examples of calling each endpoint with proper headers, body formats, and expected responses. Document rate limiting, pagination implementations, and filtering capabilities. Address common integration patterns, client-side usage examples, and troubleshooting guides for API consumers. Provide guidance on extending existing routers and creating new endpoint groups following established conventions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Request Workflow API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for resource request workflow endpoints. Document request submission, tracking, and lifecycle management. Detail request status transitions from pending to approved/rejected. Include request history and audit trail retrieval. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods (POST /api/v1/requests, GET /api/v1/requests, GET /api/v1/requests/{id}, PATCH /api/v1/requests/{id}/status), URL patterns with request IDs, request/response schemas with request details, template references, and approval chain information. Authentication should enforce user-specific request creation and admin-level status updates. Provide examples of request submission with template selection, status update workflows, and paginated request lists with filtering options. Document error handling for invalid templates, insufficient permissions, and workflow state transitions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Deployment Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed production deployment documentation for the ECS Creator platform. Document step-by-step deployment procedures for various hosting environments including cloud platforms, on-premises servers, and container orchestration systems. Explain database initialization, migration execution, and data seeding processes. Include scaling strategies, high availability setup, and disaster recovery planning. Detail monitoring and alerting configuration, backup procedures, and maintenance tasks. Provide performance tuning recommendations, security hardening guidelines, and operational runbooks for common scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the service layer abstraction pattern and business logic organization. Explain how services encapsulate complex business operations, handle external API integrations, and manage transaction boundaries. Detail the Alibaba Cloud ECS and VPC service implementations including error handling, retry logic, and rate limiting. Document the approval workflow engine, settings management service, and service composition patterns. Include examples of creating new services, implementing external integrations, and testing service layers with mocks. Address error propagation, logging strategies, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build System & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the frontend build system configuration and development environment setup. Explain Vite configuration for development server, build optimization, and code splitting. Detail Tailwind CSS configuration for styling customization, theme setup, and responsive design utilities. Document PostCSS configuration for CSS processing and autoprefixing. Include package.json scripts for development, testing, and production builds. Provide guidance on extending the build pipeline, adding custom plugins, and optimizing bundle size.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Administration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the template administration interface. Document the Templates component features including template creation, editing, versioning, validation, and deployment workflows. Explain the template editor interface, configuration options, preview functionality, and template sharing mechanisms. Include examples of creating complex templates, managing template versions, validating template configurations, and distributing templates to users.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cryptographic Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for cryptographic utilities and security services. Detail encryption/decryption functions, hashing algorithms, and secure random generation methods. Document key management practices, salt generation for password hashing, and secure comparison operations. Explain integration with external crypto libraries and configuration options for different security levels. Include examples of encrypting sensitive data, generating secure tokens, and implementing custom cryptographic operations. Address security best practices, algorithm selection criteria, and performance considerations for cryptographic operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Approval System API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the approval system endpoints. Document HTTP methods for managing approval workflows including approval request creation, approval actions (approve/reject), escalation handling, and approval history retrieval. Explain approval chain configuration, conditional approvals, and multi-level approval processes. Include examples of approval workflow setup, automated approval rules, and notification triggers. Document approval state management, conflict resolution, and audit logging integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Migrations & Schema Evolution
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the Alembic migration strategy and schema evolution process. Explain migration file structure, version control for database schemas, and rollback procedures. Detail the environment configuration, script templates, and migration generation workflows. Include examples of creating new migrations, handling schema changes, and managing production deployments. Address best practices for safe schema evolution, data preservation, and testing migration scripts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Approval System API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the approval workflow management endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas for managing approval chains, processing approvals/rejections, and tracking approval status. Include examples of multi-level approval workflows, escalation handling, and notification triggers. Explain the integration with request workflows and user permissions. Address approval state transitions, audit logging, and bulk approval operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all RESTful endpoints in the ECS Creator platform. Document authentication endpoints including login, logout, and token refresh mechanisms. Detail user management APIs for CRUD operations and role assignments. Cover template management endpoints for resource configuration definitions. Document request submission and approval workflow APIs. Include active resources monitoring endpoints and audit log retrieval. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and status codes. Provide concrete examples of request/response payloads and error handling patterns. Address rate limiting, versioning, and security considerations for API consumers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build System & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the frontend build system and configuration files. Explain the Vite build configuration including development server setup, production builds, asset optimization, and plugin configurations. Document the Tailwind CSS configuration for custom themes, responsive breakpoints, and utility class customization. Cover PostCSS processing pipeline, package dependencies management, and build scripts. Include guidance for optimizing build performance, adding new dependencies, and configuring environment-specific builds.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer & Business Logic
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the service layer implementing core business logic. Thoroughly explain the Alibaba Cloud ECS and VPC service integrations including instance provisioning, network configuration, and resource lifecycle management. Document the approval workflow engine covering request routing, state transitions, and notification systems. Detail the settings service for application configuration management and persistence. Include concrete examples of service method invocation, error handling patterns, and retry mechanisms. Address asynchronous operation handling, rate limiting for cloud APIs, and monitoring strategies. Provide guidance on extending services with new cloud providers or business workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Approval Workflow API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for approval workflow endpoints. Document approval request management including creation, review, and decision endpoints. Detail multi-level approval chains and escalation rules. Include approval notifications and audit logging. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods (POST /api/v1/approvals, GET /api/v1/approvals, PUT /api/v1/approvals/{id}/decision), URL patterns with approval IDs, request/response schemas with approval details, decision makers, and workflow states. Document approval workflow states, timeout handling, and notification triggers for pending approvals.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Routing & Request Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the FastAPI routing system and request processing pipeline. Explain route organization patterns, dependency injection in routers, and request/response lifecycle management. Document all API endpoint groups including users, templates, requests, approvals, active resources, audit logs, and settings. Detail request validation using Pydantic schemas, response formatting, and error handling strategies. Include examples of implementing new endpoints, adding authentication guards, and organizing complex route hierarchies. Address API versioning, documentation generation, and performance monitoring at the routing level.

wiki_catalogs.name: Approval Workflow Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the approval workflow management interface. Document the Approvals component functionality including request review, approval/rejection workflows, escalation handling, and workflow monitoring. Explain the approval queue management, batch operations, notification systems, and audit logging for approval decisions. Include examples of configuring approval chains, handling complex approval scenarios, and monitoring workflow performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Password Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for password management and validation services. Document password hashing algorithms, salt generation, and secure password storage mechanisms. Detail password validation rules including complexity requirements, length constraints, and common password detection. Explain password reset functionality, temporary token generation, and secure password change workflows. Include examples of implementing custom password validators, integrating with password strength checkers, and handling password migration scenarios. Address security considerations for password handling, brute force protection, and compliance with password policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Resource Monitoring API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for active resource monitoring endpoints. Document HTTP methods for retrieving resource status, health checks, performance metrics, and resource utilization data. Explain real-time monitoring capabilities, alerting thresholds, and resource lifecycle tracking. Include examples of resource discovery, status polling, and bulk resource operations. Document filtering and search capabilities, pagination for large resource sets, and integration with monitoring dashboards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models & Database
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the ECS Creator database schema. Include examples of request/response schema design, nested object validation, and conditional validation scenarios. wiki_catalogs.name: Resource Monitoring API

For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods (GET /api/v1/resources, GET /api/v1/resources/{id}, GET /api/v1/resources/{id}/metrics), URL patterns with resource identifiers, request/response schemas with resource specifications, status indicators, and performance metrics. Explain log entry structure, metadata fields, and compliance reporting features. Include examples of audit query patterns, export functionality, and integration with security information systems. wiki_catalogs.name: Audit Logging API

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the audit log administrative interface. <blog>

# ECS Creator - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

ECS Creator is a cloud infrastructure management platform designed to streamline the creation and administration of Alibaba Cloud Elastic Compute Service (ECS) instances. The system provides a comprehensive web-based interface for managing cloud resources, user access control, approval workflows, and audit logging.

### Purpose Statement
The primary goal of ECS Creator is to simplify cloud resource provisioning through an intuitive web interface while maintaining proper governance through approval workflows and comprehensive audit trails.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Simplified Resource Management**: Provide an easy-to-use interface for creating and managing ECS instances
- **Governance and Control**: Implement approval workflows for resource provisioning
- **Security and Compliance**: Maintain detailed audit logs and user access controls
- **Template-Based Provisioning**: Support reusable configurations for consistent deployments
- **Multi-User Support**: Enable team collaboration with role-based access control

### Target Audience
- DevOps teams managing cloud infrastructure
- Development teams requiring on-demand computing resources
- System administrators overseeing cloud resource allocation
- Organizations needing governed cloud resource provisioning

## 2. Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern full-stack architecture with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Nginx Reverse Proxy]
    B --> C[React Frontend]
    B --> D[FastAPI Backend]
    D --> E[Database Layer]
    D --> F[Alibaba Cloud API]
    D --> G[Session Storage]
    
    subgraph "Frontend Layer"
        C
    end
    
    subgraph "Backend Services"
        D
        H[Auth Middleware]
        I[Service Layer]
    end
    
    subgraph "Data Layer"
        E[(PostgreSQL)]
        G[(Redis Sessions)]
    end
    
    subgraph "External Services"
        F[Alibaba Cloud ECS/VPC]
    end
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Architecture:**
- React-based single-page application with Vite build system
- Tailwind CSS for responsive UI design
- Component-based architecture with shared UI components
- Role-based routing for admin and user interfaces

**Backend Architecture:**
- FastAPI framework for high-performance REST API
- SQLAlchemy ORM for database operations
- Alembic for database migrations
- JWT-based authentication with session management
- Modular service layer for business logic

**Infrastructure Components:**
- Nginx reverse proxy for load balancing and SSL termination
- Docker containerization for consistent deployment
- PostgreSQL for persistent data storage
- Redis for session management

### Design Patterns

The project implements several key design patterns:

1. **Repository Pattern**: Database operations abstracted through models
2. **Service Layer Pattern**: Business logic separated from API endpoints
3. **Middleware Pattern**: Cross-cutting concerns like authentication
4. **Schema Validation**: Pydantic models for request/response validation
5. **Dependency Injection**: Clean separation of concerns in services

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Application:**
- `backend/app/main.py` - FastAPI application initialization and middleware setup
- `backend/entrypoint.sh` - Container startup script with environment configuration

**Frontend Application:**
- `frontend/src/main.jsx` - React application bootstrap
- `frontend/vite.config.js` - Build configuration and development server setup

### Core Modules

**Authentication & Authorization:**
- `backend/app/middleware/auth.py` - JWT token validation and user context injection
- `backend/app/routers/auth.py` - Authentication endpoints and user management
- `backend/app/schemas/auth.py` - Authentication request/response schemas

**Resource Management:**
- `backend/app/services/aliyun_ecs.py` - Alibaba Cloud ECS instance management
- `backend/app/services/aliyun_vpc.py` - Virtual Private Cloud operations
- `backend/app/routers/active_resources.py` - Active resource monitoring and management

**Approval Workflow:**
- `backend/app/services/approval.py` - Approval workflow engine
- `backend/app/routers/approvals.py` - Approval request management
- `backend/app/schemas/approval.py` - Approval request schemas

**Audit & Logging:**
- `backend/app/models/audit_log.py` - Audit log data model
- `backend/app/routers/audit.py` - Audit trail access and reporting

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration:**
- `backend/app/config.py` - Centralized configuration management
- `backend/alembic.ini` - Database migration settings
- `docker-compose.yml` - Multi-container orchestration

**Build & Deployment:**
- `backend/Dockerfile` - Backend container image definition
- `frontend/Dockerfile` - Frontend container image definition
- `nginx/nginx.conf` - Reverse proxy and static file serving

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies:**
- FastAPI - High-performance web framework
- SQLAlchemy - SQL toolkit and ORM
- PyJWT - JSON Web Token implementation
- Aliyun SDK - Alibaba Cloud API integration
- Alembic - Database migration tool

**Frontend Dependencies:**
- React - UI component library
- Vite - Next-generation frontend tooling
- Tailwind CSS - Utility-first CSS framework
- Axios - HTTP client for API communication

### Integration Points

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User Interface
    participant API as FastAPI Backend
    participant Auth as Auth Service
    participant Cloud as Alibaba Cloud API
    participant DB as Database
    
    User->>+API: Create ECS Request
    API->>+Auth: Validate User Session
    Auth-->>-API: User Context
    API->>+DB: Store Request
    API->>+Cloud: Provision Resources
    Cloud-->>-API: Resource Details
    API->>+DB: Update Request Status
    API-->>-User: Confirmation
```

**Component Relationships:**

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[API Router Layer] --> B[Service Layer]
    B --> C[Data Models]
    B --> D[External APIs]
    C --> E[(Database)]
    D --> F[Alibaba Cloud]
    
    G[Auth Middleware] --> A
    H[Request Schemas] --> A
    I[Audit Logger] --> B
```

Sources:
- [main.py](backend/app/main.py)
- [auth.py](backend/app/middleware/auth.py)
- [aliyun_ecs.py](backend/app/services/aliyun_ecs.py)
- [approval.py](backend/app/services/approval.py)
- [config.py](backend/app/config.py)
- [main.jsx](frontend/src/main.jsx)
- [vite.config.js](frontend/vite.config.js)
- [Dockerfile](backend/Dockerfile)
- [docker-compose.yml](docker-compose.yml)

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**User Management & Authentication:**
- Multi-user support with role-based access control
- Secure JWT-based authentication
- User profile management and permissions
- Admin panel for user administration

**Resource Provisioning:**
- Template-based ECS instance creation
- Automated resource lifecycle management
- Real-time resource status monitoring
- Batch operations for multiple instances

**Approval Workflow System:**
- Configurable approval chains
- Email notifications for pending approvals
- Approval history and audit trail
- Escalation rules for urgent requests

**Audit & Compliance:**
- Comprehensive audit logging
- Resource change tracking
- User activity monitoring
- Compliance reporting capabilities

### Implementation Highlights

**Template Management:**
- `backend/app/models/template.py` - Reusable resource configuration templates
- `backend/app/routers/templates.py` - Template CRUD operations
- `backend/app/schemas/template.py` - Template validation schemas

**Active Resource Monitoring:**
- `backend/app/routers/active_resources.py` - Real-time resource status
- `backend/app/services/aliyun_ecs.py` - Cloud resource synchronization
- `frontend/src/pages/admin/ActiveResources.jsx` - Resource dashboard

**Settings Management:**
- `backend/app/services/settings_service.py` - Application configuration
- `backend/app/routers/settings.py` - Settings API endpoints
- `backend/app/models/settings.py` - Persistent settings storage

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Pending: New Request
    Pending --> Approved: Admin Approval
    Pending --> Rejected: Admin Rejection
    Approved --> Provisioning: Start Creation
    Provisioning --> Active: Success
    Provisioning --> Failed: Error
    Active --> Terminating: Delete Request
    Terminating --> [*]: Complete
    Rejected --> [*]: End
    Failed --> Pending: Retry
```

**Approval Workflow States:**

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Submits Request] --> B[Validation Check]
    B -->|Valid| C[Create Approval Request]
    B -->|Invalid| D[Return Error]
    C --> E[Notify Approvers]
    E --> F{Approval Decision}
    F -->|Approved| G[Provision Resources]
    F -->|Rejected| H[Reject Request]
    G --> I[Update Status]
    H --> I
    I --> J[Send Notifications]
```

Sources:
- [template.py](backend/app/models/template.py)
- [templates.py](backend/app/routers/templates.py)
- [ActiveResources.jsx](frontend/src/pages/admin/ActiveResources.jsx)
- [settings_service.py](backend/app/services/settings_service.py)
- [settings.py](backend/app/routers/settings.py)
- [approval.py](backend/app/services/approval.py)

</blog>
